arts.ttl: Revamped Villa Store to open this weekend
arts.sum: Aston Villa is delighted to announce that the revamped Villa Store will be fully open this weekend. The improved ‘Claret, Blue and Brand New’ Villa Park store will open its doors at 9am on Saturday (April 19) and comes complete with a brand-new entrance and an external wrap-around screen featuring cutting edge forced perspective video. What’s more, the store now includes a grab-and-go refreshments station as well as a green screen photo experience, adding to the fantastic shopping experience for Villans of all ages. From Saturday, May 3, the revamped ticket office will also open in this space. The first 150 attendees on Saturday will receive a free claret and blue scarf, there will be 40% off training wear while home, away and third shirts will all be available to purchase ahead of next Saturday’s FA Cup semi-final at Wembley Stadium plus much more ahead of our trip to the capital. With excitement building ahead of next weekend, why not bring the family along and make use of the fantastic offers available in-store this Saturday?

arts.ttl: Newcastle United issue big Sven Botman injury update after fears star's season was over
arts.sum: Tindall said: "Certainly not this weekend. But Tindall is confident that Botman will play again this season after earlier reports his campaign had been completely curtailed.

arts.ttl: Man Utd ace has been ‘unfairly treated’ as Alan Shearer raises major Marcus Rashford issue
arts.sum: Rasmus Hojlund has struggled in front of goal for Manchester United in recent weeks - but Alan Shearer believes the Danish striker has been "unfairly treated" at Old Trafford

arts.ttl: Sverre Nypan breaks silence as he responds to Aston Villa transfer
arts.sum: Aston Villa are said to be trying to sign Rosenborg talent Sverre Nypan Sverre Nypan breaks silence as he responds to Aston Villa transfer

arts.ttl: Anthony Gordon warned amid bold Newcastle United decision with writing on the wall
arts.sum: "I think it's testament to all these players that weren't starting games and weren't in the team for a long period of time," Tindall added of Barnes' recent performances. "At times like this as a player, as frustrating as it can be sometimes, you do need to put the shoe on the other foot and go 'right, OK, if I was that player performing that way, what would I expect?' You would always say that you would expect to be a good teammate, to support that person, to continue to work hard every single day so when your moment does come you're able to go out there and deliver the performances that everybody knows you can."

arts.ttl: The truth behind Aston Villa's Champions League blueprint as 'crucial' point explained
arts.sum: Villa will now turn their focus to securing a return to UEFA’s elite club competition as they host Newcastle United at Villa Park on Saturday (5:30pm), followed by a clash with another Champions League contender, Manchester City, on Tuesday night. Aston Villa are targeting a top five finish this season to return to the Champions League

arts.ttl: Villa trio nominated for FC25 Team of the Season
arts.sum: Three Aston Villa players have been nominated for the EA Sports FC25 Premier League Team of the Season. Morgan Rogers, Ollie Watkins and Youri Tielemans are all included in the shortlist for the side, voted for by the fans, with Villa supporters able to pick their team and select the trio now. For Rogers, it’s been a standout first full season in the top flight for the Villans. The 22-year-old has racked up eight goals and eight assists and counting in the league this term, with 26 goal involvements in 47 games across all competitions to earn him a first senior England call-up and four international appearances. Watkins has again enjoyed a successful campaign, not just in front of goal himself, but also when assisting team-mates. The England forward has reached 20 Premier League goal involvements for the third successive season, with 14 goals and six assists so far in the league, and a further goal and six more assists in all competitions. And ever-present Tielemans has proved a mainstay in the centre of Villa’s midfield all season, starting each and every one of the club's 32 Premier League games so far, along with each Champions League and FA Cup outing to date, scoring four goals and registering nine assists in all competitions. You can now pick your EA Sports FC25 Premier League Team of the Season – and vote for Morgan, Ollie and Youri – by clicking below. EA Sports FC Premier League Team of the Season Vote Now

arts.ttl: Newcastle United await Eddie Howe health decision ahead of Ipswich Town game
arts.sum: The club released a statement on Monday stating illness-stricken Howe would miss the games against Crystal Palace and Aston Villa. Eddie Howe remains on the road to recovery after being diagnosed with pneumonia and has kept contact with assistant manager Jason Tindall minimal.

arts.ttl: PSG could face UEFA punishment over Marcus Rashford incident during Champions League clash
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain beat Aston Villa 5-4 on aggregate to reach the semi-finals of the Champions League, but UEFA could sanction them after items were thrown at Marcus Rashford Paris Saint-Germain could be sanctioned by UEFA after Marcus Rashford had missiles thrown at him during their Champions League clash with Aston Villa on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Newcastle join the race to sign €25-30 million-rated German ace
arts.sum: Newcastle United are looking to improve their defensive unit in the summer, and they have identified Malick Thiaw as a potential target. The 23-year-old German defender has done quite well in Italy, and he could prove to be a quality addition for Newcastle.
